subject: COAS Web Brief - May 18, 2026
paragraph: Broken links have climbed to 1,354 across College web properties, Nolan is going to confirm with Sondra that we can get a student worker listing up and get these worked on ASAP. This week, the site drew 526,500 new users across 792,700 sessions, with Mathematics leading all departments at 83,021 sessions, yet a 36% engagement rate and a 2:55 average session duration suggest too many visitors are not finding what they came for. Our mobile score of 60, ten points below our own target of 70, compounds the problem at a moment when the majority of first-time visitors arrive on a phone. The web team will prioritize a analyzing data and planning out a structured link-audit sprint and mobile-performance fix.
priorities:
? Working with David and Patrick to place Robots.txt files in AWS to hide dev-site pages
? (as well as) working on access to Google Search Console from Patrick to hide links in GSC as well.
? Planning on discussing web strategy document draft with Kailynn in 1:1 on Tuesday.
? Placing markers on clarity to look at student journey data in clarity for Dr. Barge Project.
? Considering content strategy for statistic listings per web task force meeting on friday.
note: Reminder: Meeting with Jeremy Tarpley, Wednesday afternoon, adding considerations from Frank to conversation.
